title: Missing window state persistence (size, position, maximized)
body:
## Problem
The BrowserWindow in `src/main/index.ts:14-30` is initialized with hardcoded dimensions (1440x900) every launch. Daja does not persist or restore window bounds, maximized state, or multi-monitor position. Users reset to default size/position every session.

## Evidence
- `src/main/index.ts:14-30` — hardcoded width/height, no restoration logic

## Expected
App serializes window bounds (x, y, width, height) + maximized state on close/before-quit, restores on startup, handles missing-monitor gracefully.

## Proposed fix
1. Persist bounds to JSON in userData dir on `window-all-closed` / `before-quit`
2. Validate restored coords are still on an available display before applying
3. Consider `electron-window-state` package for battle-tested implementation
4. Fall back to centered default if persisted state is corrupt

## Impact
Every launch feels like a fresh install; power users lose their preferred layout.

title: No application menu bar — macOS feels broken
body:
## Problem
No `Menu.setApplicationMenu()` call exists. `autoHideMenuBar: true` (`src/main/index.ts:21`) hides what doesn't exist anyway. On macOS, missing File/Edit/View/Window/Help menus makes the app feel like a half-baked web wrapper. Windows/Linux users can't discover standard actions (Quit, Preferences, About).

## Evidence
- `src/main/index.ts:21` — autoHideMenuBar flag, no menu template
- No `src/main/menu.ts` file

## Expected
Platform-aware menu template with standard items + custom Daja actions (New Trade, Import CSV, etc.) wired to keyboard shortcuts.

## Proposed fix
Create `src/main/menu.ts` with platform-sensitive template. On macOS include app menu (About, Preferences, Services, Hide, Quit). Cross-platform File/Edit/View/Window/Help. Wire shortcuts (Cmd/Ctrl+Q, Cmd/Ctrl+,). Send IPC events to renderer for app actions.

## Impact
macOS users perceive the app as unprofessional; discoverability of power features is zero.

title: Single-instance lock not enforced — DB corruption risk
body:
## Problem
`src/main/index.ts` never calls `app.requestSingleInstanceLock()`. Users can launch multiple instances, each holding a better-sqlite3 handle to `daja.db`. SQLite serialized-mode works on single connection only; concurrent processes writing cause corruption or missed writes.

## Evidence
- `src/main/index.ts:46-66` — app.on('ready') with no lock check
- better-sqlite3 used in `src/main/db/client.ts`

## Expected
Second-launch focuses existing window; only one process holds the DB.

## Proposed fix
```ts
const ok = app.requestSingleInstanceLock()
if (!ok) { app.quit(); process.exit(0) }
app.on('second-instance', () => { mainWindow?.isMinimized() && mainWindow.restore(); mainWindow?.focus() })
```
Place before `app.whenReady()`.

## Impact
Double-click or Spotlight re-launch can silently corrupt trade history.

title: No custom protocol handler (daja://) for deep links
body:
## Problem
Daja does not register a custom protocol. External deep links (email, briefing, notifications) cannot open a specific ticker / watchlist / trade.

## Evidence
- `src/main/index.ts` — no `app.setAsDefaultProtocolClient` call
- No `open-url` handler

## Expected
`daja://ticker/AAPL` focuses main window on AAPL detail; `daja://watchlist/tech` opens that watchlist.

## Proposed fix
1. Register protocol in main
2. Handle `open-url` (macOS) + `second-instance` argv (Win/Linux)
3. Parse URL with a shared router and send IPC event to renderer
4. Update electron-builder.yml protocols section for installer registration

## Impact
Email briefings and system notifications can't deep-link into app — every click dumps user at home.

title: Auto-update not configured (placeholder publish URL)
body:
## Problem
`electron-builder.yml:41-43` references `https://example.com/auto-updates` as the publish endpoint. No `electron-updater` integration in main process. Users have no path to receive security fixes.

## Evidence
- `electron-builder.yml:41-43` — placeholder URL
- No `autoUpdater` import anywhere in `src/main/`

## Expected
App checks for updates on startup (with opt-out), downloads in background, prompts install on restart.

## Proposed fix
1. Publish to GitHub Releases via electron-builder publish config
2. `import { autoUpdater } from 'electron-updater'`, call `autoUpdater.checkForUpdatesAndNotify()` after `whenReady`
3. Handle `update-downloaded` to prompt user
4. Settings toggle for auto-install behavior
5. Required: code-signing certificate for signed delta updates

## Impact
Users stuck on initial version; critical CVE in Electron runtime can't be pushed.

title: macOS notarize disabled — Gatekeeper blocks downloads
body:
## Problem
`electron-builder.yml:28` has `notarize: false`. Unsigned/unnotarized .app triggers Gatekeeper warning on macOS 10.15+; users must right-click → Open and acknowledge security prompt.

## Evidence
- `electron-builder.yml:28` — notarize: false
- No code-signing identity configured

## Expected
Releases are signed with Apple Developer ID and notarized. Download + open works with zero security prompts.

## Proposed fix
1. Apple Developer Program membership
2. Store certificate + notarization credentials in CI secrets
3. Set `notarize: true` + provide `APPLE_ID`, `APPLE_ID_PASS`, `APPLE_TEAM_ID` env
4. Sign Windows installer with code-signing cert
5. CI workflow runs `electron-builder --mac --publish always`

## Impact
First-time users encounter scary warning → low conversion; auto-update also depends on signing.

title: Notifications lack action buttons and rich metadata
body:
## Problem
`src/main/ipc/systemIpc.ts:19-31` creates bare `Notification({title, body})`. No action buttons, icon, sound, urgency, or reply affordance. For a trading app where notifications cover price alerts, earnings, trade fills — this is thin.

## Evidence
- `src/main/ipc/systemIpc.ts:19-31` — minimal construction

## Expected
Actions (View Trade / Dismiss / Snooze), ticker icon or app logo, sound toggle per category, click handler routes to correct screen.

## Proposed fix
Extend IPC payload: `{title, body, icon?, actions?: {label, id}[], urgency?: 'low'|'normal'|'critical'}`. Wire `n.on('action', (ev, idx) => mainWindow.webContents.send('notification:action', actionId))`. Renderer listens + navigates via hash router.

## Impact
Notifications are info-only; user can't triage from the tray.

title: No before-quit / will-quit / window-close lifecycle hooks
body:
## Problem
`src/main/index.ts:68-71` only handles `window-all-closed`. No `before-quit`, no `will-quit`, no per-window `close` intercept. Background AI streams + DB-flushing work can be cut mid-flight.

## Evidence
- `src/main/index.ts:68-71` — only window-all-closed + closeDatabase
- No SIGTERM / SIGINT handlers

## Expected
Graceful shutdown flushes DB, aborts pending AI streams, persists window state, optionally prompts on unsaved journal edits.

## Proposed fix
1. `app.on('before-quit', async (e) => { await flushPending(); })` with e.preventDefault() until done
2. `mainWindow.on('close', (e) => persistWindowState())`
3. SIGTERM/SIGINT → graceful app.quit
4. Ensure `closeDatabase()` is idempotent

## Impact
Force-quit during streaming journal autosave can leave JSON store half-written; DB WAL may not checkpoint cleanly.

title: npmRebuild disabled breaks better-sqlite3 across archs
body:
## Problem
`electron-builder.yml:40` sets `npmRebuild: false`. better-sqlite3 ships precompiled ABI; packaging on ARM and distributing to x64 Mac users produces `dlopen … wrong architecture` crash on startup.

## Evidence
- `electron-builder.yml:40` — npmRebuild: false
- `package.json` pnpm.onlyBuiltDependencies includes better-sqlite3 (acknowledges rebuild need)

## Expected
Each target arch packages with matching native binary; optionally build universal binary on macOS.

## Proposed fix
Either flip `npmRebuild: true` (slower CI but correct), or explicit `electron-rebuild -f -w better-sqlite3` step in build script. Consider matrix build (macos-13 x64, macos-14 arm64) in CI.

## Impact
Any non-matching-arch install crashes on first DB access.

title: Linux desktop integration incomplete (no MimeType, generic maintainer)
body:
## Problem
`electron-builder.yml:31-39` declares AppImage/snap/deb targets but maintainer is `electronjs.org` placeholder, no `desktop:` metadata, no MimeType registration for .pdf → PDF module won't appear as a handler in file managers.

## Evidence
- `electron-builder.yml:31-39` — minimal linux section
- No icon files referenced for Linux app menu entries

## Expected
Full .desktop entry with Name/Comment/Categories/Keywords/MimeType; snapcraft.yml for snap channel; icons at 16/32/64/128/256/512.

## Proposed fix
Add `linux.desktop: { Name, Comment, Categories: 'Utility;Finance;Office', MimeType: 'application/pdf;' }`, real maintainer email, provide `build/icon.png` + PNG pyramid. Test on Ubuntu + Fedora + Arch.

## Impact
Linux users install but app doesn't show in launcher or handle PDFs — poor first impression.
